zh-tw.title: 畢業語錄
zh-tw.description: 從搞笑到感人，從告別到出發——精選畢業季最有共鳴的語錄，適合IG、LINE分享或送給即將踏入新階段的你

en.title: Graduation Quotes
en.description: Funny, heartfelt, and real — a curated collection of graduation quotes for captions, messages, and moments that mark the end of one chapter and the start of the next

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
終於畢業了。

這代表我終於可以換一個地方，
繼續過著不確定自己在幹嘛的生活。

但至少換了一個新地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給剛畢業的朋友或貼到IG，比嚴肅的畢業祝賀更接地氣，也更讓人有共鳴——尤其是那些還不知道畢業後要幹嘛的人
variations: 畢業是人生的一個里程碑，里程碑之後你會發現：路還是一樣要繼續走，只是換了一條不認識的。

en.content:
Finally graduated.

Which means I can now move on to being confused
in an entirely new setting.

Progress.
en.editorNote: Post this on Instagram or send to a freshly graduated friend — more relatable than formal congratulations, especially for the ones who still have no idea what comes next
variations: Graduation is a milestone. After the milestone, you realize the road still goes on — just into territory you've never seen before.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
告別的，不只是學校。

是那個每天都有人等你的早晨。
是那個在教室外面傳訊息說「你到了嗎」的人。
是那段不用解釋就知道你在想什麼的時光。

以後還是可以聯絡，但那個「每天」，真的結束了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在畢業典禮後傳給一起走過這段路的朋友，或是貼在IG配上畢業照，引發深刻共鳴
variations: 最難說再見的不是地方，是那個在那個地方、只屬於那段時間的我們。

en.content:
You're not just saying goodbye to a school.

You're saying goodbye to the mornings when someone was always waiting for you.
To the message that said "are you here yet?" before class.
To the kind of silence that didn't need explaining.

You can still keep in touch. But the 'every day' part — that's really over.
en.editorNote: Send to the friends who walked through it with you, or post with a graduation photo — hits differently than a formal farewell
variations: The hardest goodbye isn't the place. It's the version of us that only existed there.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
「我們以後一定要常聯絡！」
「一定要！」

——六個月後——

「……你還好嗎？」
「對，你呢？」
「還好。」
「好。」
「好。」

友情就這樣傳承下去了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個你在畢業典禮上說「要常聯絡」的朋友，誰先傳誰就贏了
variations: 有一種友情叫做：明明好幾個月沒說話，但只要其中一個人先傳，就馬上回到熟悉的頻率。

en.content:
"We have to keep in touch!"
"Definitely!"

— Six months later —

"...Hey, you doing okay?"
"Yeah. You?"
"Yeah."
"Cool."
"Cool."

And that's friendship, carried forward.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend you said 'let's keep in touch' to at graduation — whoever texts first wins
variations: There's a kind of friendship where months can pass in silence, but the moment one of you reaches out, you're both immediately back at the same frequency.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作（社畜era）

zh-tw.content:
恭喜你，畢業了。

學生era正式結束。
社畜era即將解鎖。
中間這段不知道要幹嘛的era，
請好好珍惜。

它通常很短，而且不會再來了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在畢業典禮前後傳給同學，也可以貼到IG，「In My ___ Era」格式在2026是超流行的IG caption風格
variations: In My 剛畢業什麼都不確定 Era — 這是你可以用的IG caption，同時也是事實陳述。

en.content:
Congratulations, you graduated.

Student era: officially closed.
Working adult era: unlocking.
The in-between era where you have no idea what you're doing:
please treasure it.

It's usually short. And it doesn't come back.
en.editorNote: Post at graduation season — the 'In My ___ Era' format is a dominant 2026 Instagram caption style and this version nails it
variations: In My Freshly Graduated Everything Is Uncertain Era — both a valid IG caption and an accurate description of your current state.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
世界很大。

先出去走走再說。

不用現在就知道要去哪裡，
只要先邁出去就好。
方向，走著走著就清楚了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些畢業後還在猶豫、還不確定下一步的人——不說教，只是給一個出發的理由
variations: 你不需要有計畫才能開始，只需要一個不想繼續待在原地的理由。

en.content:
The world is big.

Go explore first, figure it out later.

You don't need to know where you're going right now.
Just take the first step.
The direction gets clearer as you walk.
en.editorNote: For graduates who are hesitating or uncertain about what comes next — not preachy, just a reason to start moving
variations: You don't need a plan to begin. You just need a reason strong enough to stop staying where you are.

items.sourceNote: 畢業英文IG caption，重新創作

zh-tw.content: 流蘇終於值了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 翻譯自英文 caption 名句「The tassel was worth the hassle」，簡短有力，配上學士帽照片效果一流
variations: 四年的努力，換一頂帽子和一張紙。但那頂帽子，拍起來真的很好看。

en.content: The tassel was worth the hassle.
en.editorNote: One of the most shareable graduation captions of all time — works as a standalone photo caption or printed on a card
variations: Four years for a piece of paper and a hat. The hat photographs really well, though.

items.sourceNote: 畢業英文IG caption，重新創作

zh-tw.content: 沒在開玩笑，我真的畢業了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 輕鬆不做作的畢業po文，適合不想說大道理但還是想記錄這一刻的人，英文版「No cap, I actually graduated」更潮
variations: 就算我自己也不敢相信，但學校說我畢業了，我就相信學校的判斷。

en.content: No cap, I actually graduated.
en.editorNote: Casual, unfiltered, and completely relatable — perfect for people who want to mark the moment without being dramatic about it
variations: Even I didn't see this coming. But the diploma says so, so I'll take it.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
謝謝那些跟我一起熬過期末考的人。

不一定每次都考好，
但每次你傳來「你考完了嗎」的訊息，
我就覺得還有人在等我，
再爛的考試也可以撐過去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那些在考試期間一直陪著你、互相催眠說「會過的」的人——這句話比任何祝賀都更讓人感到被記住
variations: 能一起讀書不一定重要，重要的是你知道考完之後有人等你去吃東西。

en.content:
Thank you to everyone who survived finals with me.

We didn't always score well,
but every time you texted 'are you done yet?'
I knew someone was waiting.
And somehow that made even the worst exams bearable.
en.editorNote: Send to the people who spent late nights studying beside you — this lands deeper than any formal thank-you
variations: Studying together mattered less than knowing someone was waiting for you to go eat after the exam.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你知道畢業代表什麼嗎？

代表你通過了所有的考試、所有的死線、所有那些你說「這次真的沒救了」的時刻。

既然這些都過了，
剩下的人生還有什麼是過不去的？

（應該是有，但現在不用想這麼多。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 在畢業後的各種慶祝場合都能說這句，讓大家先開心一下——括號裡的那句是給想太多的人加的
variations: 你連畢業論文都熬過來了。你已經沒有什麼好怕的了。（先信這個，其他的以後再說。）

en.content:
You know what graduating means?

It means you survived every test, every deadline, every moment you said 'I'm completely done for this time.'

If you made it through all of that,
what's left in life that you can't get through?

(There are things. But you don't need to think about them right now.)
en.editorNote: Works at any graduation celebration — gives everyone a moment of genuine confidence, with the parenthetical for people who overthink everything
variations: You survived your thesis. There is nothing left to fear. (Believe this for now. Deal with the rest later.)

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業之後，你不一定知道下一步要去哪裡。

但你已經不是剛入學時的那個人了。

光是這一點，就很值得慶祝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些在畢業典禮上感覺自己還是沒想清楚的人——不需要有答案，改變本身就是答案
variations: 你不需要畢業就馬上知道人生方向。你只需要知道：你比四年前準備得更好了。

en.content:
After graduation, you may not know where you're going next.

But you are no longer the person who walked in on the first day.

That alone is worth celebrating.
en.editorNote: For the person who still doesn't have it all figured out at graduation — you don't need the answers; the change itself is the answer
variations: You don't need to know your life's direction the day you graduate. You just need to know: you're more ready than you were four years ago.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
謝謝那個在你說「我讀不下去了」的時候，
沒有叫你繼續撐，
只說了一句「先休息吧」的人。

他可能不知道，就是那一句話，
讓你撐到了今天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個陪你走過這段路的人——家人、朋友、伴侶都適用，不說教，只是讓他們知道那些小事都有被記住
variations: 有一種支持，不是激勵你，而是在你快撐不住的時候，讓你知道有人在。

en.content:
Thank you to the person who, when you said 'I can't do this anymore,'
didn't tell you to keep pushing.
They just said, 'take a break.'

They may not know it,
but that one sentence is part of why you made it here.
en.editorNote: Send to whoever kept you going — family, friends, partners — not with motivation, but with presence
variations: Some support isn't about pushing you forward. It's about letting you know someone's there when you're about to fall.

items.sourceNote: 畢業英文IG caption，重新創作

zh-tw.content: 我還在摸索，但我畢業了——這也算是一種進度。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些覺得自己畢業了但好像還沒準備好的人，比「我準備好迎接未來了」更誠實，也更有共鳴
variations: 「我不確定接下來要做什麼，但我確定我從這裡出發了」——這是一種誠實的畢業宣言。

en.content: Still figuring it out — but I graduated, so that counts as progress.
en.editorNote: For everyone who graduates without having it all figured out — more honest than 'ready to take on the world,' and more people will relate
variations: 'I don't know what comes next, but I know I'm leaving from here' — that's an honest graduation statement.

items.sourceNote: 畢業搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
畢業之後你才會知道：
學生証是這個世界上最具CP值的身分證。

半價電影、特價博物館、學生方案折扣……

然後就沒了。
請好好珍惜你還有學生証的每一天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給即將畢業的學弟妹，讓他們在最後一段學生時光充分利用學生折扣——開玩笑，但是真的
variations: 學生証的失效日，可能是你這輩子最後一次享有正版半價的那一天。慎重對待。

en.content:
After graduation, you'll understand:
A student ID is the highest-value piece of ID ever created.

Half-price movies. Discounted museums. Student plan savings...

And then it's gone.
Treasure every day you still have one.
en.editorNote: Send to underclassmen or juniors still in school — make full use of those discounts while you still can. It's a joke, but it's also real.
variations: Your student ID's expiry date may be the last time you get legal half-price anything. Treat it with respect.

items.sourceNote: 畢業感人語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
謝謝那些陪我一起迷路的朋友。

我們不是沒有走錯路，
但至少是一起走錯的。

而且每次走錯，都多認識了彼此一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那些跟你一起選錯課、走錯教室、繞錯路、甚至一起選錯方向的人——那些「一起錯」的時刻，往往才是最難忘的
variations: 不一定要走對路才叫成長，有時候走錯路、但有人一起走，才是這段時間最值錢的部分。

en.content:
Thank you to the friends who got lost with me.

We didn't always take the right turns.
But at least we took the wrong ones together.

And every wrong turn taught us a little more about each other.
en.editorNote: Send to the ones who took the wrong class with you, got confused at the same crossroads, went in the wrong direction at the same time — those 'wrong together' moments are often the most unforgettable
variations: Growth doesn't always require the right path. Sometimes taking the wrong one together is the most valuable part of the whole journey.

items.sourceNote: 畢業勵志搞笑語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
不知道前面是什麼。

但不出發，就永遠不知道。

先走，邊走邊想，
這是目前所有畢業生共同採用的策略。
而且到目前為止，大家都還活著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給所有站在起點、對未來既興奮又不安的畢業生——輕鬆幽默但真心鼓勵，最後一句是廢話文學bonus
variations: 畢業的意義不是你準備好了，而是你決定出發了。這兩件事不一定要同時發生。

en.content:
Nobody knows what's ahead.

But you won't find out by staying.

So start moving. Think while you walk.
This is the shared strategy of every graduate, everywhere.
And so far, everyone has survived.
en.editorNote: For every graduate standing at the starting line feeling both excited and terrified — light humor with genuine encouragement, with a bonus absurd-wisdom final line
variations: Graduating doesn't mean you're ready. It means you decided to go anyway. Those two things don't have to happen at the same time.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Parade 畢業語錄合集，改寫為台灣口語版本

zh-tw.content:
在離開之前，要好好感謝那些默默陪你撐過來的人：

Google。
Ctrl+F。
還有那個幫你找到答案的神奇「複製貼上」。

沒有你們，今天的我不會站在這裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在畢業典禮上唸這段，或是傳給同學當最後的共同回憶——每個人都懂那種靠Google活過考試的感覺
variations: 畢業感言可以很誠實：謝謝所有我在考試前一天發現的YouTube教學影片。

en.content:
Before you go, take a moment to thank those who silently carried you through:

Google.
Ctrl+F.
And the sacred art of copy and paste.

Without you, I would not be standing here today.
en.editorNote: Read this at a graduation party or send it to your study group as a final shared memory — everyone has survived on Google at some point
variations: An honest graduation speech: thank you to every YouTube tutorial I found the night before the exam.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Shutterfly 畢業語錄，改寫為台灣口語風格

zh-tw.content:
很多人在畢業的時候問：「然後呢？」

但其實你不需要現在就知道「然後」。

你只需要知道你想去的那個方向，
然後往那裡走。

「然後呢」，會在路上自己出現的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些在畢業典禮上還是不知道下一步的人——不是說教，只是換一個方式看這個問題
variations: 人生的下一步不一定要現在想清楚。有時候，先動起來，答案才會跟得上你。

en.content:
Everyone asks at graduation: "So what's next?"

But you don't need to know the answer right now.

You just need to know which direction you want to face,
and start walking that way.

The 'what's next' will show up on its own.
en.editorNote: For anyone who still doesn't have an answer at graduation — not a lecture, just a different way to look at the same question
variations: You don't have to have the next step figured out. Sometimes you have to start moving before the answer can catch up with you.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自台灣畢業祝賀語合集，改寫為更有溫度的版本

zh-tw.content:
你走進來的時候，什麼都不確定。

你走出去的時候，大概還是什麼都不確定。

但有一件事不一樣了——
你知道自己撐得過去。

這是這幾年給你最值錢的東西。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那些還在懷疑自己準備好了沒的畢業生——這句話不給建議，只是提醒他們已經做到了什麼
variations: 學歷不是畢業給你的最重要的東西。那個「我曾經以為不行，但還是撐過去了」的記憶，才是。

en.content:
You walked in uncertain about everything.

You're walking out still uncertain about most things.

But one thing has changed —
you now know you can make it through.

That's the most valuable thing these years gave you.
en.editorNote: For graduates who still doubt whether they're ready — this doesn't give advice, it just reminds them what they've already proven
variations: The most important thing graduation gave you isn't the degree. It's the memory of thinking you couldn't — and doing it anyway.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 TinTint 畢業金句文章，改寫為更口語自然的台灣用語

zh-tw.content:
有一種人，
你們不會每天說話，
不會固定約出來，
甚至有時候幾個月都不聯絡。

但只要其中一個人說：「最近怎樣？」
就好像沒有消失過一樣。

願你畢業之後，身邊還有這種人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個你不常聯絡但每次聯絡都像昨天才見面的人——有時候說出來比什麼都更重要
variations: 不是每一段友情都需要每天確認。真正深的那種，沉默幾個月再回來，還是同一個頻率。

en.content:
There's a kind of person
you don't talk to every day,
never have a standing date with,
sometimes go months without a word.

But the moment one of you says, 'Hey, how have you been?'
it's like no time passed at all.

Hope you still have someone like that after graduation.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend you don't talk to constantly but who always picks up right where you left off — saying it out loud sometimes matters more than anything
variations: Not every friendship needs daily check-ins. The real ones go quiet for months and come back on exactly the same frequency.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Shikshanation 畢業語錄合集，改寫為搞笑現實風格

zh-tw.content:
歡迎來到真實世界。

這裡沒有學期。
沒有寒暑假。
考試沒有固定時間，
而且大部分都沒有標準答案。

別擔心——
其實也沒有人知道這裡的規則，
大家都是邊玩邊學的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在畢業派對上說這段，或是傳給剛進社會的朋友——第一句是威嚇，後面幾句才是真正的安慰
variations: 真實世界最大的祕密是：大部分的大人也不確定自己在做什麼，只是裝出來的比你熟練一點。

en.content:
Welcome to the real world.

No semesters here.
No summer break.
The tests come without warning
and most of them don't have a correct answer.

Don't worry though —
nobody actually knows the rules.
Everyone is making it up as they go.
en.editorNote: Say this at a graduation party or send it to a friend entering the workforce — the opening line is a scare, the rest is the real comfort
variations: The biggest secret about the real world: most adults are just as uncertain as you are. They're just better at looking like they aren't.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 2026 IG 畢業 caption 趨勢，改寫為原創口語版本

zh-tw.content:
劇情大轉折：我真的畢業了。

學位到手。
人生載入中……

（附帶說明：徵才請洽本人。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 三段式IG caption，搭配學士帽照片效果超好——從驚喜到宣告到搞笑求職，一鏡到底
variations: 學位：已解鎖。接下來要解鎖什麼，等我想到再說。

en.content:
Plot twist: I actually graduated.

Degree: secured.
Life: loading…

(P.S. — please hire me.)
en.editorNote: Three-beat IG caption that pairs perfectly with a cap-toss photo — surprise, announcement, and a self-aware job-search punchline all in one
variations: Degree: unlocked. Whatever comes next: TBD. But I graduated, so we're off to a solid start.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Canvas Discount 畢業語錄，改寫為台灣口語感人版本

zh-tw.content:
這張畢業證書上只有我的名字，
但你們都知道這不是只有我一個人的事。

謝謝你們每一次沒問我為什麼回來這麼晚，
每一次假裝沒看到我眼睛紅紅的，
每一次不問直接去幫我煮了一碗東西。

這張紙，也有你們的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在畢業典禮上傳給家人，或是貼到IG tag父母——比「感謝爸媽」四個字更有份量，也更讓人鼻酸
variations: 有些人從頭到尾沒有出現在任何一張課堂照片裡，但如果沒有他們，我不會走到這一步。這句話，是給他們的。

en.content:
Only my name is on this diploma.
But you all know this wasn't just me.

Thank you for every time you didn't ask why I came home so late.
For every time you pretended not to notice my eyes were red.
For every time you just went ahead and made me something to eat without saying a word.

This piece of paper belongs to you too.
en.editorNote: Send to family at graduation or post and tag your parents — more weight than just 'thanks mom and dad,' and more likely to make someone tear up
variations: Some people never showed up in a single class photo, but without them I wouldn't have made it here. This one's for them.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Career Nuts 搞笑畢業語錄，改寫為台灣廢話文學風格

zh-tw.content:
我花了好幾年的學費，
換來一張紙、一頂帽子、
還有「知道自己不知道什麼」的能力。

這最後一樣，
其實是最值錢的。

（但我還是希望那頂帽子可以留著。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 這句話在典禮上說是笑點，但說完之後大家都會想一下——括號那句是給需要笑點收尾的人
variations: 大學四年教會我最重要的事，不是任何一門課的內容，而是「找到答案的方法」。還有怎麼在最後一天交報告。

en.content:
I traded years of tuition for a piece of paper, a hat,
and the ability to know what I don't know.

That last one is actually the most valuable thing I got.

(I'm still glad I get to keep the hat though.)
en.editorNote: Gets a laugh at the ceremony but leaves everyone thinking for a second — the parenthetical is for anyone who needs a lighter landing
variations: The most important thing college taught me wasn't in any syllabus. It was how to find the answer — and how to submit it the morning it's due.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Pinkoi 畢業祝福語合集，改寫為台灣年輕人口吻的勵志語錄

zh-tw.content:
有一件事沒有人告訴你：

畢業之後，
你終於可以自己選要成為什麼樣的人，
不再只是「某某學校的學生」。

這個自由有時候讓人害怕，
但它也是你等了好幾年的東西。

好好用它。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些對「不再是學生」這件事感到茫然的人——這句話不是告訴你要做什麼，而是提醒你現在的選擇是你自己的
variations: 畢業不是終點，是你第一次真正可以為自己做決定的起點。慢慢來，但記得出發。

en.content:
Here's something nobody tells you:

After graduation,
you finally get to decide who you want to become —
not just 'a student at somewhere.'

That freedom can feel scary.
But it's also what you've been waiting for.

Make the most of it.
en.editorNote: For anyone who feels unmoored now that 'being a student' is no longer their identity — this isn't telling you what to do, it's reminding you the choice is now yours
variations: Graduation isn't an ending. It's the first moment you get to make decisions that are truly your own. Take your time, but don't forget to start.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自 Minted 畢業語錄集，改寫為台灣口語感人告別語錄

zh-tw.content:
你會記得的，不是考了幾分，
也不是交了幾份報告。

是那個下雨天一起躲在走廊等放晴的下午。
是那個考完試坐在台階上什麼都不說的沉默。
是那個不知道為什麼突然就笑起來的瞬間。

這些，才是你帶走的東西。
zh-tw.editorNote: 畢業典禮當天傳給一起走過來的人，或是配上老照片貼到限時動態——這種細節感的文字比任何名言都更容易讓人紅眼眶
variations: 多年後你回想這段時光，記住的不會是那些大事，而是那些小到說不出口、卻剛好被你留下來的片刻。

en.content:
What you'll remember isn't your GPA
or how many papers you turned in.

It's the rainy afternoon you and a friend got stuck under the same overhang waiting for it to clear.
The silence after a hard exam, sitting on the steps, no words needed.
The moment something suddenly became funny for no reason at all.

Those are the things you're actually taking with you.
en.editorNote: Send on graduation day to the people who shared those quiet moments with you, or pair with an old photo for a story post — this kind of specific detail hits harder than any famous quote
variations: Years from now, the big events will blur. What stays are the small moments you didn't even know you were saving.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「畢業像軟體更新」笑話，改寫為台灣口語廢話文學版本

zh-tw.content:
畢業就像手機系統更新。

你知道遲早要來，
你也知道更新完會變更好，
但按下去的那一瞬間你還是緊張。

而且更新途中絕對不能關機。

——你現在就在更新中，請耐心等待。
zh-tw.editorNote: 很適合在畢業典禮或謝師宴上說，科技世代最懂這個比喻——最後一句讓人覺得「被理解了」的效果很強
variations: 畢業 = 強制更新。不管你準不準備好，進度條都會繼續跑。

en.content:
Graduating is like a system update on your phone.

You knew it was coming.
You know it's supposed to make things better.
But when the moment actually arrives, you're still a little nervous.

And whatever you do, don't shut down mid-update.

— You are currently updating. Please be patient.
en.editorNote: Works perfectly at a graduation party or farewell dinner — every tech-era grad instantly gets this metaphor, and the final line lands with a satisfying 'someone gets me' feeling
variations: Graduation = forced update. Ready or not, the progress bar keeps moving.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「感謝Google幫我撐過來」系列搞笑語錄，改寫為台灣版本（與gq-016不同角度，聚焦咖啡因與熬夜）

zh-tw.content:
今天站在這裡，我要感謝許多人。

感謝咖啡，讓我在最後一堂課沒有睡著。
感謝便利商店，在凌晨三點還開著門迎接我。
感謝那個不知道幾點的自己，決定再撐一下。

你們都是這張畢業證書的共同作者。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在謝師宴或畢業派對上說，有笑點也有暖點，台灣便利商店文化讓這段在本地特別有共鳴
variations: 畢業感言誠實版：謝謝咖啡、謝謝7-11、謝謝那個凌晨五點還在改報告的我。

en.content:
Today, standing here, I want to thank a few people.

Coffee, for keeping me awake through the final lecture.
The convenience store, for being open at 3 a.m. when I needed it most.
And the version of me who decided, in the dark, to keep going a little longer.

You are all co-authors of this diploma.
en.editorNote: Say this at a graduation dinner or send as a toast — gets a laugh and a nod, the 'keep going a little longer' line lands with genuine warmth
variations: Honest graduation speech: thank you to coffee, late-night snacks, and the 2 a.m. version of me who somehow believed it was going to be fine.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自2026年畢業IG caption趨勢中的Taylor Swift era風格，改寫為台灣口語版本（與gq-004 era主題不同，聚焦歌詞感）

zh-tw.content:
我把最好的年紀都給了這裡。

還有最慘的期末考、最睏的上課時光、
最多報告的學期、還有那幾頓沒吃的午餐。

但如果可以重來，
我還是選這裡。

（可能啦。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配上四年前的新生照對比畢業照——括號那句是給不想太肉麻的人準備的逃生出口，整體走懷念又帶一點幽默的路線
variations: 這裡拿走了我很多東西，但它也給了我一些永遠帶走的。我們扯平了。

en.content:
I gave some of my best years to this place.

Also my worst finals, my most sleep-deprived mornings,
my most deadline-heavy semesters, and several lunches I just skipped.

But if I could do it over,
I'd still choose here.

(Probably.)
en.editorNote: Pair with a side-by-side of your freshman and graduation photos — the parenthetical is the exit ramp for anyone who doesn't want to sound too sentimental, but the whole thing lands as nostalgic with a wink
variations: This place took a lot from me. But it gave me things I'll carry forever. I'd call it even.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自「找不到工作可以去念研究所」幽默語錄，改寫為台灣口語搞笑版本

zh-tw.content:
找不到工作怎麼辦？

去念研究所。

念完研究所還是找不到工作怎麼辦？

去念博士。

念完博士還是找不到工作怎麼辦？

恭喜你，你可以去當教授了。

人生總是有出路的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在謝師宴或畢業派對上說最好，引爆全場然後讓大家笑著想自己的下一步——最後一句是正面收尾
variations: 有一種人生規劃叫做：先畢業，再說。這也是一種計畫，而且非常多人在用。

en.content:
Can't find a job after graduation?

Go get a Master's.

Still can't find a job after that?

Go get a PhD.

Still nothing?

Congratulations — you're now qualified to become a professor.

There's always a way forward.
en.editorNote: Best delivered out loud at a graduation party — triggers a big laugh and then a moment of 'wait, is that actually my plan?' for half the room; the final line gives it an optimistic landing
variations: One valid life plan: graduate first, figure out the rest later. It's a strategy. A lot of people are using it.

items.sourceNote: 靈感來自台灣2026可愛畢業祝福語合集，改寫為有溫度的勵志短語

zh-tw.content:
畢業之後，你不會消失。

你只是移動到了另一個地方，
繼續閃著你的光。

不管在哪裡，記得讓人找得到你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給即將各奔東西的朋友，或是貼在限時動態tag所有人——簡短有力，比長篇大論更容易讓人記住
variations: 我們不是散了，只是換了地方繼續發光。記得偶爾讓我看到你在哪裡。

en.content:
After graduation, you don't disappear.

You just move to a different place
and keep shining from there.

Wherever you end up — stay findable.
en.editorNote: Send to friends scattering in different directions, or post a story and tag everyone — short and direct, the kind of thing people actually remember
variations: We're not gone. We're just shining from different spots now. Make sure I can still find yours.
